mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals expanding corporate output and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO accelerations are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent compresses equity discount rates and reflects abundant liquidity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or the mean return in these periods does not exceed the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between industrial production growth and unemployment changes amplifies business cycle risk, with the combination of weak output and labor market deterioration driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE changes fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a t-statistic magnitude below 1.5 in a 3-year rolling regression, or the joint signal produces positive returns in more than 50 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In high-unemployment regimes, positive payroll growth signals labor market healing and future consumption recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8%, positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month retail sales growth exceeding 0.5 percent signals robust consumer spending momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of RSAFS month-over-month growth above 0.5% are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of cases over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth predicts aggregate demand expansion and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth quartiles fail to monotonically predict 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Kendall tau below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month CPI inflation increases discount rates and compresses real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Top-quintile CPIAUCSL acceleration portfolios fail to underperform bottom-quintile portfolios over a 2-year rolling window (spread t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds 9%, persistent labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE above 9% are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or the mean return in these periods exceeds the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).
